perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first written above.
perturbation.changed_text: ACCESS TO STUDENT DATA: The Institution acknowledges and agrees that it will provide the Provider with access to student data solely for the purpose of enabling the Provider to provide the Services. The Institution will ensure that it has obtained all necessary consents from students or their parents or guardians, as applicable, to provide the Provider with access to student data. The Provider will use student data only for the purpose of providing the Services and will not disclose student data to any third party without the Institution’s prior written consent. The Provider will implement and maintain reasonable security measures to protect student data from unauthorized access, use, or disclosure. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first written above.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Access to Student Data' clause to the end of the contract buries it. It should be in a dedicated section related to data handling and privacy to ensure clear and conspicuous notice to all parties. This relocation makes it difficult to readily find and review the terms related to student data privacy.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA)
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving data access clauses to an inconspicuous location makes it less likely that the parties involved (especially educational institutions and students/parents) will clearly understand their rights and responsibilities under FERPA, particularly regarding consent and data security measures.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The relocation of the 'Access to Student Data' clause to the end of the contract, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, creates a structural flaw. This flaw makes it difficult to readily find and review the terms related to student data privacy, potentially contradicting the principles of FERPA. The Law Explanation states that an inconspicuous location makes it less likely that involved parties will clearly understand their rights and responsibilities regarding consent and data security measures. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is a 'Document not Found' error, Scraped Law Snippet 1 references 20 U.S.C. 1232g and 34 CFR 99, which pertain to FERPA, implying that the agreement should ensure clear understanding and adherence to data privacy regulations. The changed text weakens the prominence of these obligations, thus creating a legal contradiction under the Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction definition.
